description: Detects SageMaker real-time endpoint variants that run fixed GPU or accelerator capacity without target tracking or scheduled scaling.

severity_rules.name: missing_target_tracking_autoscaling
severity_rules.condition: production_variant_exists == true AND initial_instance_count > 1 AND no_appautoscaling_target_or_policy == true
severity_rules.description: Endpoint capacity is fixed. Add Application Auto Scaling target tracking for SageMakerVariantInvocationsPerInstance or a validated custom metric.

severity_rules.name: missing_scheduled_scaling
severity_rules.description: Endpoint capacity remains high during predictable off-hours. Add scheduled scaling to reduce to the minimum safe capacity.

severity_rules.name: gpu_endpoint_underutilized
severity_rules.condition: gpu_or_accelerator_instance == true AND gpu_utilization_avg_pct < 25 AND gpu_memory_utilization_p95_pct < 60 AND p95_latency_has_headroom == true AND errors == 0
severity_rules.description: GPU endpoint appears over-provisioned. Review smaller instance types, fewer instances, batching, or alternate inference mode.

severity_rules.name: always_on_endpoint_for_bursty_workload
severity_rules.condition: real_time_endpoint == true AND idle_or_bursty_traffic == true AND strict_low_latency_sla_absent == true
severity_rules.description: Always-on real-time endpoint may not fit workload shape. Compare async inference, serverless inference, batch transform, Bedrock, or self-hosted options.

validation_before_change: Check p95/p99 latency, model load time, errors, throttles, and GPU memory.
Use canary or shadow testing before reducing production capacity.
Keep minimum safe capacity for workloads that require 24x7 low latency.
